criteria.name: feedback timing has no leakage violations
multi-timescale memory does not regress tail accuracy versus v1.4
criteria.note: The candidate cannot buy recurrence by damaging ordinary tail performance.

criteria.name: multi-timescale memory improves recurrence or recovery
criteria.note: At least one recurrent-regime metric must improve versus v1.4.

criteria.name: memory ablations are worse than the candidate
criteria.note: No-slow/no-structural/no-BOCPD controls must not explain the benefit.

criteria.name: candidate has at least one external-baseline return-regime edge
criteria.note: This is a reviewer-defense gate, not a universal superiority claim.

result.failure_reason: Failed criteria: multi-timescale memory does not regress tail accuracy versus v1.4, multi-timescale memory improves recurrence or recovery, memory ablations are worse than the candidate
result.name: Tier 5.10 - Multi-Timescale Memory And Forgetting Diagnostic

tier_summary.claim_boundary: Diagnostic software mechanism test only; v1.4 remains frozen until a candidate passes, ablates cleanly, and survives compact regression.

summary.claim_boundary: Diagnostic software mechanism test only; v1.4 remains frozen until a candidate passes, ablates cleanly, and survives compact regression.
